patch.value:
Promoted from the pm-mpbb DX ledger (candidate lever d) on 2026-07-11, when the pm-a22j baseline gate opened.

The numbers make the case: pm contracts --summary = 2,867 B TOON (~0.7k tok) vs contracts --full = 506,091 B (176x) vs the MCP tools/list payload = 32,979 B (~8.2k tok, paid on every MCP session bootstrap). The cheap two-call cold-start already exists — `pm contracts --summary` (what exists) then `pm next` (what to do) — but no doc or plugin template teaches it as THE orientation path, so agents default to expensive discovery (--help walks, contracts --full, or full MCP attachment).

Scope: docs (agents guide + README agent section) describe the two-call cold-start as the canonical orientation; plugin instruction templates (Claude/Codex/ChatGPT alignment, see plan commit ba09b155) teach the same sequence; cite the baseline script scripts/measure-agent-token-surface.mjs for before/after numbers.

Constraint (ledger fence): no new top-level commands — this is documentation and template work only.
Document the two-call agent cold-start (contracts --summary + next) as the canonical orientation in docs and plugin templates

Agent-facing docs describe the two-call cold-start as the canonical orientation path; plugin instruction templates teach the same sequence; no new top-level commands introduced; before/after token numbers cited from the a22j baseline script

author: codex-root

patch.value: Standardize the two-call contracts-summary plus next cold start across agent docs and plugin templates with measured token evidence.

message: Backfill concise active-item definition from existing title, acceptance criteria, and live audit evidence
